name: RABBIT-CAGE-17
title: scope-guard false positive: detects write targets inside string literals
description: extract_bash_targets() in scope-guard.sh scans the full bash command string for redirect patterns without excluding content inside quoted strings. When a script contains example commands as string data (e.g. in a heredoc or Python string), the scope guard extracts those example paths as write targets and denies the tool call. The actual write destination was an allowlisted .claude/backlogs/ path; the detected path was only in a string literal. Fix: skip pattern matching inside single-quoted or double-quoted regions, or at minimum skip inside heredoc content.

history.note: Fixed: extract_bash_targets now strips quoted regions before pattern matching
